speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Is this the monument of Leonato?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: It is, my lord.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Done to death by slanderous tongues

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Was the Hero that here lies.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Death, in guerdon of her wrongs,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Gives her fame which never dies.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: So the life that died with shame

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Lives in death with glorious fame.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Hang thou there upon the tomb,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Praising her when I am dumb.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Now music, sound, and sing your solemn hymn.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Pardon, goddess of the night,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Those that slew thy virgin knight,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: For the which with songs of woe,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Round about her tomb they go.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Midnight, assist our moan.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Help us to sigh and groan

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Heavily, heavily.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Graves, yawn and yield your dead,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Till death be utterèd,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Heavily, heavily.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Now, unto thy bones, goodnight.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Yearly will I do this rite.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Good morrow, masters. Put your torches out.

speaker: PRINCE
text: The wolves have preyed, and look, the gentle day

speaker: PRINCE
text: Before the wheels of Phoebus, round about

speaker: PRINCE
text: Dapples the drowsy east with spots of gray.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Thanks to you all, and leave us. Fare you well.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Good morrow, masters. Each his several way.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Come, let us hence, and put on other weeds,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And then to Leonato’s we will go.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And Hymen now with luckier issue speed ’s,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Than this for whom we rendered up this woe.
